actionable_feedback: Add parseable exports: include CSV copies of every sheet (RawInputs.csv, Calculations.csv, DecisionTable.csv) alongside the .xlsx so graders/verifiers can read content without special libraries.

Publish a short plain-text (or CSV) final_decision_table file listing each run, decision (accept/reject), numeric values used (yield, impurity), and the exact cell citations used in the workbook (e.g., RawInputs!A2, Calculations!C5).
Embed cached (display) values next to formulas in the workbook or include a PDF snapshot of each sheet so reviewers can see computed results without relying on formula evaluation.

Update final_message.txt to include the top-line conclusions and at least one sample row-to-decision citation (e.g., 'Run2 decision: Reject - impurity 12.3% (RawInputs!B3) used by formula Calculations!D3 -> DecisionTable!A2').

If using a custom builder script (build_formulation_screen.py), ensure the produced OOXML is compatible with common readers (openpyxl/libreoffice) or additionally produce CSV/PDF outputs to guarantee previewability.
artifact_specific_comments: formulation_screen.xlsx present and claims to include raw inputs, intermediate calculations, validation checks, needs_review notes, and exact row/value citations, but preview failed with: 'No module named \"openpyxl\"'.
Because the grader could not parse the .xlsx, semantic checks relied on the attempt metadata and logs; include CSV/PDF/text exports to allow full automated verification.
feedback_summary: The baseline produced the required three-sheet formulation_screen.xlsx and accompanying logs. Deterministic precheck passed (artifact present). However, the grader could not programmatically open/inspect the .xlsx (preview error: openpyxl unavailable), so semantic verification was partial (0.75). To obtain full credit and allow automated verification, deliverables must include parseable exports and an explicit, human-readable mapping from final decisions to exact cell citations.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

raw_llm_output.feedback_summary: The baseline produced the required three-sheet formulation_screen.xlsx and accompanying logs. Deterministic precheck passed (artifact present). However, the grader could not programmatically open/inspect the .xlsx (preview error: openpyxl unavailable), so semantic verification was partial (0.75). To obtain full credit and allow automated verification, deliverables must include parseable exports and an explicit, human-readable mapping from final decisions to exact cell citations.
raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Regenerate the deliverables for 160-revised by exporting each worksheet as CSV, adding a plain-text final_decision_table with explicit cell citations, and including a PDF snapshot of the workbook. Re-run verification so the grader can programmatically confirm formulas, citations, and decision logic.
trace_specific_comments: Trace documents a fallback from an unavailable artifact-tool to a custom OOXML builder (build_formulation_screen.py). Fallback is acceptable, but it increases the need for parseable exports to enable verification.
Agent trace confirms final summary: all three runs fail impurity rule; Run2 and Run3 meet yield but fail impurity. Ensure the revised artifact explicitly shows these values with cell citations.
